mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals expanding labor income that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following month-over-month PAYEMS increases are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the Rank IC between PAYEMS change and 20-day returns falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) of more than 0.3 percentage points signals rapid labor market deterioration that reduces household income and corporate earnings expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the mean return in triggered windows is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month growth in industrial production (INDPRO) and decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) jointly signal a broad-based recovery in output and labor markets that produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts independently.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO up × UNRATE down) does not add incremental explanatory power for 20-day returns in a joint regression (t-stat of interaction coefficient < 1.96), or the mean return in the joint condition does not exceed the mean return in the single-condition groups (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is below 0.25%, month-over-month increases in consumer prices (CPIAUCSL) signal successful monetary reflation that erodes real debt burdens and boosts nominal corporate revenues, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, month-over-month CPIAUCSL increases fail to produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns (mean return <= 0 over triggered windows) or the strategy Sharpe ratio over triggered periods is below 0.2.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in advance retail sales (RSAFS) captures contemporaneous consumer spending momentum that directly translates into revenue surprises for consumer-facing S&P 500 firms, predicting positive 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The rank correlation between month-over-month RSAFS changes and 5-day S&P 500 forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (Spearman p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A two-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) exceeding 1% signals a contraction in manufacturing and mining output that precedes negative earnings revisions for industrial and materials sectors, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following triggered INDPRO declines are positive in more than 50% of instances, or the average return in triggered windows is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) crossing above 600 thousand units signals a durable recovery in residential investment and construction employment, which stimulates demand for building materials and consumer durables and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 600, S&P 500 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 50% of instances or the mean return over those windows is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) exceeding 200 thousand persons signals severe labor market contraction that crushes consumer confidence and spending, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns following triggered PAYEMS declines are positive in more than 50% of instances.
